perturbation.original_text: g. Supply of Endorsed Products. Company shall deliver to North, at no charge, and in a timely fashion, a reasonable number of Endorsed Products for North's personal use each Contract Year during the Contract Period. All postage expenses associated with the delivery of the Endorsed Products shall be borne by Company.
perturbation.changed_text: g. Supply of Endorsed Products. Company shall deliver to North, at no charge, only if available, a reasonable number of Endorsed Products for North's personal use each Contract Year during the Contract Period. All postage expenses associated with the delivery of the Endorsed Products shall be borne by North.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the supply of endorsed products. The addition of 'only if available' makes the obligation of the company conditional, contradicting the earlier statement 'Company shall deliver'. Furthermore, shifting the responsibility for postage expenses to North contradicts the initial agreement. The in-text contradiction arises because paragraph 7.a specifies a 'Guaranteed Service Fee,' implying guaranteed services and benefits, which are now undermined by conditional product supply and shifted expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Compensation. a. Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer. In consideration for the rights, services and benefits granted by North hereunder, Company agrees to pay North a non-refundable Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer (hereinafter referred to as "Guaranteed Fee") of fifty-five thousand dollars ($55,000USD) in Contract Year 2011 and seventy thousand dollars ($70,000USD) in Contract Year 2012.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a contradiction because it makes the supply of endorsed products conditional ('only if available') and shifts postage expenses to North. This directly conflicts with paragraph 7.a, which guarantees a 'Guaranteed Service Fee and Marketing Retainer' in consideration for the rights, services, and benefits granted by North. The original text guaranteed the delivery of products, while the changed text makes it uncertain, thus undermining the guaranteed benefits outlined in paragraph 7.a, which is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: b. Golf Bag. Use and carry Company's F2 branded golf bag at all Public Events as defined immediately above. North shall have the right to place a maximum of two (2) non-competitive third party sponsor names/logos that are of a reasonable size on the belly portion of the F2 branded golf bag.
perturbation.changed_text: b. Golf Bag. Use and carry Company's F2 branded golf bag at some Public Events as defined immediately above. North shall have the right to place a maximum of two (2) competitive third party sponsor names/logos that are of a reasonable size on the belly portion of the F2 branded golf bag.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires North to use the golf bag at 'all' public events, but the modified version changes this to 'some' public events, creating ambiguity about which events require using the bag. Further, the modification allows for 'competitive' third-party logos, which would directly contradict paragraph 3 that grants the company 'exclusivity' regarding endorsements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Grant of License and Exclusivity. Subject to the terms set forth in this Agreement, North hereby grants to Company the right and privilege to use North's Likeness and North's Endorsement during the Term and within the Contract Territory in all reasonable forms of advertising including, but not limited to television (including the infomercial format), radio, print advertising, brochures, pamphlets, product packaging, point-of-purchase materials, Company's web-site and a demonstration video (hereinafter referred to as "Advertising Materials") in connection with Company's advertisement and sale of the Endorsed Product only. During the Term of this Agreement and within the Contract Territory, North agrees not to enter into an agreement with another company or entity for the purpose of endorsing or promoting products similar to the Endorsed Products.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces two contradictions. First, changing 'all Public Events' to 'some Public Events' creates ambiguity regarding when the golf bag must be used, potentially conflicting with implicit or explicit expectations in other sections concerning North's promotional obligations. Second, allowing 'competitive' third-party logos on the golf bag directly contradicts paragraph 3, which grants the company exclusivity in endorsements, creating a clear conflict of interest and potentially diminishing the value of the company's endorsement rights.

perturbation.original_text: North agrees that any Advertising Materials submitted for approval as provided herein may be deemed by Company to have been approved hereunder if the same is not disapproved by North within seven (7) business days after receipt thereof. North agrees that any Advertising Materials submitted hereunder will not be unreasonably disapproved and, if it is disapproved, that Company will be advised of the specific grounds for the disapproval. Company shall not use such disapproved Advertising Materials until it has written approval from North.
perturbation.changed_text: North agrees that any Advertising Materials submitted for approval as provided herein may be deemed by Company to have been approved hereunder if the same is not disapproved by North within fourteen (14) calendar days after receipt thereof. North agrees that any Advertising Materials submitted hereunder may be unreasonably disapproved and, if it is disapproved, that Company is not required to be advised of the specific grounds for the disapproval. Company shall use such disapproved Advertising Materials until it receives written disapproval from North.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces multiple points of contradiction within section 8 regarding the approval of advertising materials. The extension of the approval period to fourteen calendar days contradicts the initial seven business days. Further, allowing for unreasonable disapproval and removing the requirement to provide specific grounds directly conflicts with ensuring fair and transparent dealings, which goes against section 11 of 'Standards'. In addition, it states that 'Company shall use such disapproved Advertising Materials until it receives written disapproval from North.', contradicting earlier requirements for written approval.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Standards. Company and North shall at all times deal with each other in good faith and strive to maintain and enhance each other's positive image and reputation. North and Company shall act at all times in a manner that shall not be in contravention of public morals and conventions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions. First, it extends the approval period for advertising materials from seven business days to fourteen calendar days, which can lead to different interpretations and potential conflicts. Second, it allows for unreasonable disapproval of advertising materials and removes the requirement to provide specific grounds, which directly conflicts with the principle of dealing in good faith and maintaining a positive image as outlined in Section 11. Third, it states that the Company can use disapproved materials until written disapproval is received, contradicting any implied or explicit requirement for approval before use. These changes introduce ambiguity and inconsistency, weakening the agreement's enforceability and violating the standard of good faith dealings outlined in the contradicted text.
